seo.title: AP Psych Score Calculator 2026 | Estimate Your AP Psychology Score
seo.description: Use this AP Psych Score Calculator to estimate your AP Psychology exam score from 1 to 5. Enter your MCQ and FRQ scores to predict your AP Psych score.

content.directAnswer: Use this AP Psych Score Calculator to estimate your AP Psychology score from 1 to 5 based on your multiple-choice and free-response performance.

sections.heading: How to Use the AP Psych Score Calculator
sections.body: Enter your multiple-choice correct answers and your two FRQ scores. The calculator estimates your composite percentage and converts it into an estimated AP score from 1 to 5.

sections.heading: How AP Psychology Scoring Works
sections.body: This calculator combines MCQ and FRQ performance into a weighted composite estimate. It is designed for planning and post-exam estimation, not as an official score report.

sections.heading: What Score Do You Need to Get a 5 on AP Psych?
sections.body: A strong estimated range for a 5 is around 75% or higher on the composite estimate, but actual thresholds may vary by year.

faqs.question: Is this calculator official?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator is an independent estimate and is not affiliated with or endorsed by College Board.

faqs.question: Can actual AP score cutoffs vary?
faqs.answer: Yes. Actual score thresholds can vary by year and exam form, so this tool should be used for planning only.

faqs.question: Does FRQ performance matter for AP Psych?
faqs.answer: Yes. FRQ performance contributes to the composite estimate, so a stronger FRQ score can offset some missed multiple-choice questions.

faqs.question: Should I use this before or after the exam?
faqs.answer: You can use it before the exam for target planning or after the exam to estimate possible score outcomes.
